summary:
A plain retraction cord should be chosen for a hypertensive patient as it avoids unnecessary vasodilation that may increase blood pressure; options like those containing epinephrine are more dangerous given this consideration. 
This version maintains focus while ensuring both critical aspects—vasoactivity and its implications—are succinctly conveyed within two sentences.
